merged_results.title: Plan and list target facts + likely authoritative sources

result.title: Plan and list target facts + likely authoritative sources

details.text: Internal plan: Target facts needed: (1) Exact GitHub repository counts for the top 5 languages in 2024 (raw counts), (2) Exact repository counts for the same languages in 2023, (3) Percentage change (2024 vs 2023) for each language, (4) Primary use cases for each language (short authoritative citations), (5) Identify which language had the largest percentage growth, (6) Trend analysis and reasoned 2-year prediction for whether any language will overtake the current leader. Likely sources to find repo counts: GitHub's official reports (State of the Octoverse / GitHub blog), GitHub Archive/GitHub public data, GHTorrent or other dataset pages, and reputable trackers (RedMonk, TIOBE) for corroboration. For primary use cases: official language docs, Wikipedia summaries, and Stack Overflow / industry surveys. Verification steps: (A) search for GitHub 2024 language rankings and visit top official pages to extract exact counts and source URLs, (B) search for GitHub 2023 language counts and visit to extract counts, (C) cross-check counts if possible (GitHub vs third-party datasets) and record visited URLs, (D) gather primary-use citations for each language, (E) compute percentage changes and identify largest growth, (F) analyze trends (adoption, ecosystem growth, major frameworks, corporate backing, education) using citations (Stack Overflow, Octoverse, RedMonk), then produce prediction with reasoning and cite all visited URLs. Measurable outputs expected: for each top-5 language — {language, 2024_repo_count (int), 2023_repo_count (int), pct_change (float %), primary_use_cases (list + citations)}; plus {language_with_largest_pct_growth, trend_analysis_text, prediction_with_confidence_and_reasons, all citations (visited URLs)}. This reduces uncertainty by prioritizing official GitHub data first, then corroborating with third-party datasets and authoritative language docs.

results.title: Octoverse: A new developer joins GitHub every second as AI leads TypeScript to #1 - The GitHub Blog
results.description: It remains the clear leader inside AI-tagged repositories, where Jupyter Notebook usage nearly doubled in 2025 offering evidence of its role as the go-to language for prototyping, training, and orchestrating AI workloads. Typed > loosely typed. TypeScript’s growth confirms our 2024 observation: much of what was previously counted as “JavaScript” activity already came through TypeScript transpilation pipelines.

results.title: r/cpp on Reddit: Octoverse 2025 Github survey is out
results.description: Position 5 is C# :) 80% repos are not C++, but Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, C++, and C# together. This year C++ alone grow 11.82% (~1.7 mln repos). Also check this image ...

results.title: TypeScript Tops GitHub Octoverse as AI Era Reshapes Language Choices -- Visual Studio Magazine
results.description: Aug 2024) to control for seasonality. C# Growth Remains Solid: C# ranks No. 5 in the language table by YoY contributor gains, adding 136,735 contributors (22.22% YoY, Aug-over-Aug). In the core-stacks view of new projects, C# appears among the six languages used by nearly 80% of new repositories, with 1,478,463 repositories and 10.61% growth over the past year.

results.title: GitHub’s 2025 Report Reveals Some Surprising Developer Trends
results.description: This year's report shows TypeScript overtaking Python and JavaScript as the most used programming language, while India overtook the US in total open source contributor count for the first time.

results.title: Octoverse: The state of open source and rise of AI in 2023 - The GitHub Blog
results.description: 2023: a year in this report is the last 365 days from the last Octoverse release and ranges from 10/1/2022 to 9/30/2023. For each language, provide: the exact repository count, the percentage change from 2023, the primary use cases, and identify which language had the largest percentage growth. Then analyze trends and predict which language might overtake the current leader in the next 2 years, with reasoning. Cite all data sources.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

reasons: Agent output contains only a plan to fetch data, not the requested results.

No language was identified as the growth leader (largest percentage increase).
No trend analysis or 2-year prediction with reasoning was provided.
No data sources or citations were included.
